MustParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) {\n\tresult := p.ParseFile(filePath, data)\n\tif !result.Success {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to parse YAML file %s: %v\", filePath, result.Error))\n\t}\n}\n\n// ParseString parses YAML content from a string into the provided data structure.\n// The data parameter must be a pointer to the target structure.\nfunc (p *Parser) ParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error {\n\treturn yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yamlContent), data)\n}\n\n// Exists checks if a file exists at the given path.\nfunc Exists(filePath string) files in a directory (non-recursive).\nfunc FindYAMLFiles(dirPath string) ([]string, error) {\n\tentries, err := os.ReadDir(dirPath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read directory: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar yamlFiles []string\n\tfor _, entry := range entries {\n\t\tif entry.IsDir() {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tfilePath := filepath.Join(dirPath, entry.Name())\n\t\tif IsYAMLFile(filePath) {\n\t\t\tyamlFiles = append(yamlFiles, filePath)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn yamlFiles, nil\n}\n\n// FindYAMLFilesRecursive finds all YAML files in a directory recursively.\nfunc FindYAMLFilesRecursive(dirPath string) ([]string, error) {\n\tvar yamlFiles []string\n\n\terr := filepath.Walk(dirPath, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {\n\t\tif err != nil to walk directory: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn yamlFiles, nil\n}\n\n// YAMLParseError represents a YAML syntax error with detailed location information.\ntype YAMLParseError struct {\n\tFilePath string\n\tLine     int\n\tColumn   int\n\tMessage  string\n\tRawError error\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ype *YAMLParseError) Error() string {\n\tif ype.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"YAML syntax error in %s at line %d: %s\", ype.FilePath, ype.Line, ype.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"YAML syntax error in %s: %s\", ype.FilePath, ype.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ype *YAMLParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ype.RawError\n}\n\n// ParseYAML reads and parses a YAML file into a generic map structure.\n// This function provides comprehensive error handling with the following behavior:\n//   - Returns map[string]interface{} and error\n//   - Distinguishes between file I/O errors and YAML syntax errors\n//   - Handles empty files gracefully (returns empty map, not error)\n//   - Provides helpful error messages with line numbers for syntax errors\n//   - Uses the file I/O foundation from file.go\n//\n// Returns an empty map for empty or whitespace-only files.\n// Returns YAMLParseError for YAML syntax errors with line/column information.\n// Returns FileError for file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.).\nfunc ParseYAML(filePath string) (map[string]interface{}, error) {\n\t// Use the existing ReadFile function to leverage file I/O error handling\n\tcontent, err := ReadFile(filePath)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// ReadFile already returns FileError with proper context\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for empty file or whitespace-only content\n\tcontentStr := string(content)\n\tif len(contentStr) == 0 || isWhitespace(contentStr) {\n\t\t// Return empty map for empty files - not an error\n\t\treturn make(map[string]interface{}), nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse YAML content with detailed error handling\n\tvar data map[string]interface{}\n\tif err := yaml.Unmarshal(content, &data); err != nil {\n\t\t// Create detailed YAMLParseError with line information\n\t\treturn nil, &YAMLParseError{\n\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\t\tMessage:  err.Error(),\n\t\t\tRawError: err,\n\t\t\tLine:     extractErrorLine(err),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Ensure we only whitespace characters.\nfunc isWhitespace(s string) bool {\n\tfor _, r := range s {\n\t\tif !isWhitespaceRune(r) {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// isWhitespaceRune checks if a rune is a whitespace character.\nfunc isWhitespaceRune(r rune) bool {\n\treturn r == ' ' || r == '\\t' || r == '\\n' || r == '\\r'\n}\n\n// extractErrorLine attempts to extract line number from YAML parser errors.\n// The yaml.v3 parser sometimes includes line information in error messages.\nfunc extractErrorLine(err error) int {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn 0\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to parse line number from common error patterns\n\t// yaml.v3 errors often contain \"line %d\" patterns\n\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\n\t// This is a best-effort extraction since yaml.v3 doesn't always provide line info\n\t// The parser typically includes line numbers in syntax errors\n\tvar line int\n\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(errMsg, \"yaml: line %d:\", &line); err == nil && line > 0 {\n\t\treturn The module is designed with three-layer architecture focusing on robust file I/O, flexible parsing, and detailed error reporting.\n6\t\n7\t## Design Principles\n8\t\n9\t1. **Safety First**: All operations return detailed error information with context\n10\t2. **Type Safety**: Provide both generic and type-safe access patterns\n11\t3. **Debugging Support**: Comprehensive error messages with line/column information\n12\t4. **Progressive Enhancement**: Support simple use cases while enabling advanced scenarios\n13\t5. **Performance**: Efficient file I/O with minimal allocations\n14\t\n15\t## Architecture Layers\n16\t\n17\t### Layer 1: File I/O Foundation (file.go)\n18\t\n19\t**Purpose**: Provide safe, contextualized file operations with detailed error reporting.\n20\t\n21\t**Key Components**:\n22\t- `FileError`: Structured error type with operation context\n23\t- `ReadFile()`: Safe file reading with error wrapping\n24\t- `FileExists()`: Reliable file existence checking\n25\t- Error classification utilities\n26\t\n27\t**Design Rationale**: \n28\t- Wraps OS errors with operation context for better debugging\n29\t- Distinguishes between \"not found\", \"permission denied\", and other I/O errors\n30\t- Provides absolute path resolution for consistent error messages\n31\t\n32\t### Layer 2: Core Parsing (parser.go)\n33\t\n34\t**Purpose**: Transform YAML content into Go data structures with comprehensive error handling.\n35\t\n36\t**Key Components**:\n37\t- `Parser`: Main parsing abstraction with strict/non-strict modes\n38\t- `ParseResult`: Structured result type with success/failure information\n39\t- `YAMLParseError`: Detailed syntax errors with line/column information\n40\t- `ParseYAML()`: Simplified parsing function for common use cases\n41\t\n42\t**Design Patterns**:\n43\t- **Builder Pattern**: `NewParser()` and `NewStrictParser()` constructors\n44\t- **Result Pattern**: `ParseResult` struct encapsulates success/failure states\n45\t- **Error Chain**: `YAMLParseError` wraps underlying parser errors\n46\t\n47\t### Layer 3: Validation & Analysis (validator.go)\n48\t\n49\t**Purpose**: Validate YAML syntax and structure with detailed error reporting.\n50\t\n51\t**Key Components**:\n52\t- `Validator`: Validation engine with configurable strictness\n53\t- `ValidationResult`: Comprehensive validation results with errors/warnings\n54\t- `ValidationError`: Detailed error with categorization\n55\t- Structural issue detection (duplicate keys, etc.)\n56\t\n57\t**Design Rationale**:\n58\t- Separates validation from parsing for focused error reporting\n59\t- Categorizes errors by type (syntax, structure, I/O, etc.)\n60\t- Provides both errors and warnings for nuanced feedback\n61\t\n62\t### Layer 4: Field Access & Debug Helpers (debug_helpers.go)\n63\t\n64\t**Purpose**: Provide convenient, type-safe access to nested YAML fields.\n65\t\n66\t**Key Components**:\n67\t- Field access functions: `GetField()`, `GetString()`, `GetInt()`, `GetBool()`\n68\t- Required field functions: `GetRequiredString()`, `GetRequiredInt()`, etc.\n69\t- Field validation: `ValidateRequiredFields()`, `ValidateFieldRequirements()`\n70\t- Error types: `FieldNotFoundError`, `TypeMismatchError`\n71\t\n72\t**Navigation Pattern**:\n73\t```go\n74\t// Dot-notation navigation: \"server.port\" → data[\"server\"][\"port\"]\n75\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n76\tfor _, part := range parts {\n77\t    currentMap, ok := current.(map[string]interface{})\n78\t    if !ok { return nil, false }\n79\t    current, ok = currentMap[part]\n80\t}\n81\t```\n82\t\n83\t## Component Relationships\n84\t\n85\t```\n86\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n87\t│                    Application Code                      │\n88\t│  (config loading, debug file processing, validation)    │\n89\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n90\t                           │\n91\t                           ▼\n92\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n93\t│              Field Access Layer (debug_helpers.go)       │\n94\t│  Type-safe field access, required field validation       │\n95\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n96\t                           │\n97\t           ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐\n98\t           ▼                               ▼\n99\t┌──────────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────────┐\n100\t│ Validation Layer     │      │ Parsing Layer        │\n101\t│ (validator.go)       │      │ (parser.go)           │\n102\t│ Syntax/structure     │      │ YAML → Go structs     │\n103\t└──────────────────────┘      └──────────────────────┘\n104\t           │                               │\n105\t           └───────────────┬───────────────┘\n106\t                           ▼\n107\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n108\t│              File I/O Layer (file.go)                    │\n109\t│  Safe file operations with error context                │\n110\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n111\t                           │\n112\t                           ▼\n113\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n114\t│              Operating System / Filesystem               │\n115\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n116\t```\n117\t\n118\t## Error Handling Strategy\n119\t\n120\t### Error Type Hierarchy\n121\t\n122\t```\n123\terror\n124\t├── FileError (file.go)\n125\t│   ├── Operation: \"read\", \"resolve\"\n126\t│   └── Underlying: OS errors (not found, permission, etc.)\n127\t├── YAMLParseError (parser.go)\n128\t│   ├── Line/Column information\n129\t│   └── Raw parser error\n130\t├── ValidationError (validator.go)\n131\t│   ├── Type: syntax, structure, io, empty\n132\t│   └── Context information\n133\t└── Field Access Errors (debug_helpers.go)\n134\t    ├── FieldNotFoundError\n135\t    └── TypeMismatchError\n136\t```\n137\t\n138\t### Error Propagation Pattern\n139\t\n140\t```go\n141\t// Layer 1: File I/O wraps OS errors\n142\treturn &FileError{\n143\t    Op:   \"read\",\n144\t    Path: absPath,\n145\t    Err:  wrapFileError(err),\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\t// Layer 2: Parser wraps parse errors\n149\treturn &YAMLParseError{\n150\t    FilePath: filePath,\n151\t    Message:  err.Error(),\n152\t    RawError: err,\n153\t    Line:     extractErrorLine(err),\n154\t}\n155\t\n156\t// Layer 3: Validator categorizes errors\n157\treturn ValidationError{\n158\t    Type:     categorizeError(errMsg),\n159\t    Line:     extractLineNumber(err),\n160\t    Message:  err.Error(),\n161\t}\n162\t```\n163\t\n164\t## Memory Management\n165\t\n166\t### Data Flow\n167\t\n168\t1. **File Reading**: `ReadFile()` loads entire file into memory\n169\t2. **Parsing**: `yaml.Unmarshal()` parses into `map[string]interface{}`\n170\t3. **Field Access**: Navigation creates minimal temporary allocations\n171\t4. **Validation**: Uses yaml.Node for efficient structure analysis\n172\t\n173\t### Optimization Considerations\n174\t\n175\t- **Lazy Parsing**: Parse only when needed, not on file discovery\n176\t- **Reuse**: Parser instances can be reused for multiple files\n177\t- **Streaming**: Not currently supported (design limitation)\n178\t- **Caching**: Not implemented (future enhancement consideration)\n179\t\n180\t## Thread Safety\n181\t\n182\t### Current Design\n183\t- **Not Thread-Safe**: Parser and Validator instances maintain state\n184\t- **Shared Data**: Parsed `map[string]interface{}` is not safe for concurrent writes\n185\t- **Recommended Pattern**: Create separate instances per goroutine\n186\t\n187\t### Future Enhancements\n188\t- Thread-safe parsers for concurrent file processing\n189\t- Synchronized field access for shared configuration structures\n190\t\n191\t## Usage Patterns\n192\t\n193\t### Pattern 1: Simple Configuration Loading\n194\t\n195\t```go\n196\t// Parse into generic map\n197\tdata, err := yamlutil.ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n198\tif err != nil {\n199\t    log.Fatal(err)\n200\t}\n201\tport := yamlutil.GetInt(data, \"server.port\", 8080)\n202\t```\n203\t\n204\t### Pattern 2: Typed Configuration Structs\n205\t\n206\t```go\n207\tparser := yamlutil.NewParser()\n208\tvar config ConfigStruct\n209\tresult := parser.ParseFile(\"config.yaml\", &config)\n210\tif !result.Success {\n211\t    log.Fatal(result.Error)\n212\t}\n213\t```\n214\t\n215\t### Pattern 3: Validation-First Approach\n216\t\n217\t```go\n218\tvalidator := yamlutil.NewValidator()\n219\tresult := validator.ValidateFile(\"config.yaml\")\n220\tif result.HasErrors() {\n221\t    fmt.Println(result.ErrorSummary())\n222\t    return\n223\t}\n224\t```\n225\t\n226\t### Pattern 4: Debug File Analysis\n227\t\n228\t```go\n229\tdata, err := yamlutil.ParseYAML(\"debug.yaml\")\n230\trequired := []string{\"timestamp\", \"level\", \"message\"}\n231\tmissing := yamlutil.ValidateRequiredFields(data, required)\n232\tif len(missing) > 0 {\n233\t    log.Printf(\"Missing required fields: %v\", missing)\n234\t}\n235\t```\n236\t\n237\t## Extension Points\n238\t\n239\t### Future Enhancements\n240\t\n241\t1. **Schema Validation**: JSON Schema or custom schema support\n242\t2. **YAML Writing**: Serialize Go structs back to YAML\n243\t3. **Stream Processing**: Handle large files incrementally  \n244\t4. **Caching Layer**: Memoize frequently parsed files\n245\t5. **Field Mutation**: Safe field setting and deletion\n246\t6. **Configuration Merging**: Combine multiple YAML sources\n247\t7. **Environment Variable Expansion**: `${VAR}` substitution\n248\t8. **Include Directives**: Import other YAML files\n249\t\n250\t### Plugin Architecture\n251\t\n252\t```go\n253\t// Future: Extensible parser with middleware\n254\ttype ParserMiddleware func(ParseContext) ParseContext\n255\t\n256\tparser := NewParser()\n257\tparser.Use(ExpandEnvVars())\n258\tparser.Use(ResolveIncludes())\n259\tparser.Use(ApplyDefaults())\n260\t```\n261\t\n262\t## Testing Strategy\n263\t\n264\t### Coverage Areas\n265\t\n266\t1. **Unit Tests**: Each exported function has dedicated tests\n267\t2. **Error Paths**: Comprehensive error condition testing\n268\t3. **Integration Tests**: End-to-end parsing and validation\n269\t4. **Edge Cases**: Empty files, malformed YAML, boundary conditions\n270\t\n271\t### Test File Organization\n272\t\n273\t- `parser_test.go`: Core parsing functionality\n274\t- `validator_test.go`: Validation logic\n275\t- `file_test.go`: File I/O operations\n276\t- `debug_helpers_test.go`: Field access and validation\n277\t\n278\t## Performance Characteristics\n279\t\n280\t### Time Complexity\n281\t\n282\t- **File Reading**: O(n) where n = file size\n283\t- **Parsing**: O(n) where n = YAML content length\n284\t- **Field Access**: O(d) where d = path depth (typically 1-3)\n285\t- **Validation**: O(n) for full document analysis\n286\t\n287\t### Space Complexity\n288\t\n289\t- **Memory Usage**: ~2x file size (raw content + parsed structure)\n290\t- **Field Access**: O(1) additional memory per operation\n291\t- **Validation**: O(d) where d = document depth for recursive checks\n292\t\n293\t## Dependencies\n294\t\n295\t### External Dependencies\n296\t\n297\t- `gopkg.in/yaml.v3`: YAML parsing and encoding\n298\t\n299\t### Internal Dependencies\n300\t\n301\t- Standard library: `fmt`, `os`, `path/filepath`, `strings`\n302\t- No ARMOR-internal dependencies (self-contained module)\n303\t\n304\t## Migration Path\n305\t\n306\t### For Existing Code\n307\t\n308\t1. **Identify**: Find ad-hoc YAML parsing in codebase\n309\t2. **Replace**: Use `yamlutil.ParseYAML()` instead of direct `yaml.Unmarshal()`\n310\t3. **Enhance**: Add validation with `Validator`\n311\t4. **Type-Safe**: Migrate to typed field access functions\n312\t\n313\t### Backward Compatibility\n314\t\n315\t- Existing YAML files work without modification\n316\t- Error handling enhancement (more detailed errors)\n317\t- Performance neutral or improved\n318\t\n319\t## Security Considerations\n320\t\n321\t### Input Validation\n322\t\n323\t- **File Size**: Consider limits for extremely large files\n324\t- **Path Traversal**: `filepath.Abs()` prevents directory traversal\n325\t- **Content Sanitization**: Parser doesn't execute code (safe by design)\n326\t\n327\t### Error Information\n328\t\n329\t- **Path Disclosure**: Error messages include file paths (acceptable for internal tool)\n330\t- **Memory Limits**: No DoS protection for malicious YAML (future enhancement)\n331\t\n332\t## Monitoring and Observability\n333\t\n334\t### Metrics to Track\n335\t\n336\t- Parse success/failure rates\n337\t- Validation error patterns\n338\t- File access latency\n339\t- Memory usage for large &config)\n347\t// Logs: \"yaml_parse\", file: \"config.yaml\", success: true, duration_ms: 15\n348\t```\n349\t\n350\t## Conclusion\n351\t\n352\tThe YAML parser module provides a robust, three-layer architecture for YAML handling in ARMOR. The design prioritizes safety, type safety, and debugging support while maintaining performance and extensibility for future enhancements.\n353\t\n354\tThe module follows Go best practices with clear error handling, comprehensive testing, and logical separation of concerns across file I/O, parsing, validation, and field access layers."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e6198b15-e2e1-467d-a9ec-23d201c401d5","uuid":"2d83e6ec-2a9b-4159-a6b8-c55dddb46024","timestamp":"2026-07-09T15:52:27.867Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/ARCHITECTURE.md","content":"# YAML Parser Module Architecture\n\n## Overview\n\nThe YAML parser utility module (`internal/yamlutil`) provides comprehensive YAML handling capabilities for ARMOR debug files and configuration management. The module is designed with three-layer architecture focusing on robust file I/O, flexible parsing, and detailed error reporting.\n\n## Design Principles\n\n1. **Safety First**: All operations return detailed error information with context\n2. **Type Safety**: Provide both generic and type-safe access patterns\n3. **Debugging Support**: Comprehensive error messages with line/column information\n4. **Progressive Enhancement**: Support simple use cases while enabling advanced scenarios\n5. **Performance**: Efficient file I/O with minimal allocations\n\n## Architecture Layers\n\n### Layer 1: File I/O Foundation (file.go)\n\n**Purpose**: Provide safe, contextualized file operations with detailed error reporting.\n\n**Key Components**:\n- `FileError`: Structured error type with operation context\n- `ReadFile()`: Safe file reading with error wrapping\n- `FileExists()`: Reliable file existence checking\n- Error classification utilities\n\n**Design Rationale**: \n- Wraps OS errors with operation context for better debugging\n- Distinguishes between \"not found\", \"permission denied\", and other I/O errors\n- Provides absolute path resolution for consistent error messages\n\n### Layer 2: Core Parsing (parser.go)\n\n**Purpose**: Transform YAML content into Go data structures with comprehensive error handling.\n\n**Key Components**:\n- `Parser`: Main parsing abstraction with strict/non-strict modes\n- `ParseResult`: Structured result type with success/failure information\n- `YAMLParseError`: Detailed syntax errors with line/column information\n- `ParseYAML()`: Simplified parsing function for common use cases\n\n**Design Patterns**:\n- **Builder Pattern**: `NewParser()` and `NewStrictParser()` constructors\n- **Result Pattern**: `ParseResult` struct encapsulates success/failure states\n- **Error Chain**: `YAMLParseError` wraps underlying parser errors\n\n### Layer 3: Validation & Analysis (validator.go)\n\n**Purpose**: Validate YAML syntax and structure with detailed error reporting.\n\n**Key Components**:\n- `Validator`: Validation engine with configurable strictness\n- `ValidationResult`: Comprehensive validation results with errors/warnings\n- `ValidationError`: Detailed error with categorization\n- Structural issue detection (duplicate keys, etc.)\n\n**Design Rationale**:\n- Separates validation from parsing for focused error reporting\n- Categorizes errors by type (syntax, structure, I/O, etc.)\n- Provides both errors and warnings for nuanced feedback\n\n### Layer 4: Field Access & Debug Helpers (debug_helpers.go)\n\n**Purpose**: Provide convenient, type-safe access to nested YAML fields.\n\n**Key Components**:\n- Field access functions: `GetField()`, `GetString()`, `GetInt()`, `GetBool()`\n- Required field functions: `GetRequiredString()`, `GetRequiredInt()`, etc.\n- Field validation: `ValidateRequiredFields()`, `ValidateFieldRequirements()`\n- Error types: `FieldNotFoundError`, `TypeMismatchError`\n\n**Navigation Pattern**:\n```go\n// Dot-notation navigation: \"server.port\" → data[\"server\"][\"port\"]\nparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\nfor _, part := range parts {\n    currentMap, ok := current.(map[string]interface{})\n    if !ok { return nil, false }\n    current, ok = currentMap[part]\n}\n```\n\n## Component Relationships\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    Application Code                      │\n│  (config loading, debug file processing, validation)    │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │\n                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│              Field Access Layer (debug_helpers.go)       │\n│  Type-safe field access, required field validation       │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │\n           ┌───────────────┴───────────────┐\n           ▼                               ▼\n┌──────────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────────┐\n│ Validation Layer     │      │ Parsing Layer        │\n│ (validator.go)       │      │ (parser.go)           │\n│ Syntax/structure     │      │ YAML → Go structs     │\n└──────────────────────┘      └──────────────────────┘\n           │                               │\n           └───────────────┬───────────────┘\n                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│              File I/O Layer (file.go)                    │\n│  Safe file operations with error context                │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                           │\n                           ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│              Operating System / Filesystem               │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n## Error Handling Strategy\n\n### Error Type Hierarchy\n\n```\nerror\n├── FileError (file.go)\n│   ├── Operation: \"read\", \"resolve\"\n│   └── Underlying: OS errors (not found, permission, etc.)\n├── YAMLParseError (parser.go)\n│   ├── Line/Column information\n│   └── Raw parser error\n├── ValidationError (validator.go)\n│   ├── Type: syntax, structure, io, empty\n│   └── Context information\n└── Field Access Errors (debug_helpers.go)\n    ├── FieldNotFoundError\n    └── TypeMismatchError\n```\n\n### Error Propagation Pattern\n\n```go\n// Layer 1: File I/O wraps OS errors\nreturn &FileError{\n    Op:   \"read\",\n    Path: absPath,\n    Err:  wrapFileError(err),\n}\n\n// Layer 2: Parser wraps parse errors\nreturn &YAMLParseError{\n    FilePath: filePath,\n    Message:  err.Error(),\n    RawError: err,\n    Line:     extractErrorLine(err),\n}\n\n// Layer 3: Validator categorizes errors\nreturn ValidationError{\n    Type:     categorizeError(errMsg),\n    Line:     extractLineNumber(err),\n    Message:  err.Error(),\n}\n```\n\n## Memory Management\n\n### Data Flow\n\n1. **File Reading**: `ReadFile()` loads entire file into memory\n2. **Parsing**: `yaml.Unmarshal()` parses into `map[string]interface{}`\n3. **Field Access**: Navigation creates minimal temporary allocations\n4. **Validation**: Uses yaml.Node for efficient structure analysis\n\n### Optimization Considerations\n\n- **Lazy Parsing**: Parse only when needed, not on file discovery\n- **Reuse**: Parser instances can be reused for multiple files\n- **Streaming**: Not currently supported (design limitation)\n- **Caching**: Not implemented (future enhancement consideration)\n\n## Thread Safety\n\n### Current Design\n- **Not Thread-Safe**: Parser and Validator instances maintain state\n- **Shared Data**: Parsed `map[string]interface{}` is not safe for concurrent writes\n- **Recommended Pattern**: Create separate instances per goroutine\n\n### Future Enhancements\n- Thread-safe parsers for concurrent file processing\n- Synchronized field access for shared configuration structures\n\n## Usage Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Simple Configuration Loading\n\n```go\n// Parse into generic map\ndata, err := yamlutil.ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\nif err != nil {\n    log.Fatal(err)\n}\nport := yamlutil.GetInt(data, \"server.port\", 8080)\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Typed Configuration Structs\n\n```go\nparser := yamlutil.NewParser()\nvar config ConfigStruct\nresult := parser.ParseFile(\"config.yaml\", &config)\nif !result.Success {\n    log.Fatal(result.Error)\n}\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: Validation-First Approach\n\n```go\nvalidator := yamlutil.NewValidator()\nresult := validator.ValidateFile(\"config.yaml\")\nif result.HasErrors() {\n    fmt.Println(result.ErrorSummary())\n    return\n}\n```\n\n### Pattern 4: Debug File Analysis\n\n```go\ndata, err := yamlutil.ParseYAML(\"debug.yaml\")\nrequired := []string{\"timestamp\", \"level\", \"message\"}\nmissing := yamlutil.ValidateRequiredFields(data, required)\nif len(missing) > 0 {\n    log.Printf(\"Missing required fields: %v\", missing)\n}\n```\n\n## Extension Points\n\n### Future Enhancements\n\n1. **Schema Validation**: JSON Schema or custom schema support\n2. **YAML Writing**: Serialize Go structs back to YAML\n3. **Stream Processing**: Handle large files incrementally  \n4. **Caching Layer**: Memoize frequently parsed files\n5. **Field Mutation**: Safe field setting and deletion\n6. **Configuration Merging**: Combine multiple YAML sources\n7. **Environment Variable Expansion**: `${VAR}` substitution\n8. **Include Directives**: Import other YAML files\n\n### Plugin Architecture\n\n```go\n// Future: Extensible parser with middleware\ntype ParserMiddleware func(ParseContext) ParseContext\n\nparser := NewParser()\nparser.Use(ExpandEnvVars())\nparser.Use(ResolveIncludes())\nparser.Use(ApplyDefaults())\n```\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n### Coverage Areas\n\n1. **Unit Tests**: Each exported function has dedicated tests\n2. **Error Paths**: Comprehensive error condition testing\n3. **Integration Tests**: End-to-end parsing and validation\n4. **Edge Cases**: Empty files, malformed YAML, boundary conditions\n\n### Test File Organization\n\n- `parser_test.go`: Core parsing functionality\n- `validator_test.go`: Validation logic\n- `file_test.go`: File I/O operations\n- `debug_helpers_test.go`: Field access and validation\n\n## Performance Characteristics\n\n### Time Complexity\n\n- **File Reading**: O(n) where n = file size\n- **Parsing**: O(n) where n = YAML content length\n- **Field Access**: O(d) where d = path depth (typically 1-3)\n- **Validation**: O(n) for full document analysis\n\n### Space Complexity\n\n- **Memory Usage**: ~2x file size (raw content + parsed structure)\n- **Field Access**: O(1) additional memory per operation\n- **Validation**: O(d) where d = document depth for recursive checks\n\n## Dependencies\n\n### External Dependencies\n\n- `gopkg.in/yaml.v3`: YAML parsing and encoding\n\n### Internal Dependencies\n\n- Standard library: `fmt`, `os`, `path/filepath`, `strings`\n- No ARMOR-internal dependencies (self-contained module)\n\n## Migration Path\n\n### For Existing Code\n\n1. **Identify**: Find ad-hoc YAML parsing in codebase\n2. **Replace**: Use `yamlutil.ParseYAML()` instead of direct `yaml.Unmarshal()`\n3. **Enhance**: Add validation with `Validator`\n4. **Type-Safe**: Migrate to typed field access functions\n\n### Backward Compatibility\n\n- Existing YAML files work without modification\n- Error handling enhancement (more detailed errors)\n- Performance neutral or improved\n\n## Security Considerations\n\n### Input Validation\n\n- **File Size**: Consider limits for extremely large files\n- **Path Traversal**: `filepath.Abs()` prevents directory traversal\n- **Content Sanitization**: Parser doesn't execute code (safe by design)\n\n### Error Information\n\n- **Path Disclosure**: Error messages include file paths (acceptable for internal tool)\n- **Memory Limits**: No DoS protection for malicious YAML (future enhancement)\n\n## Monitoring and Observability\n\n### Metrics to Track\n\n- Parse success/failure rates\n- Validation error patterns\n- File access latency\n- Memory usage for large files\n\n### Logging Integration\n\n```go\n// Future: Structured logging support\nparser.SetLogger(logger)\nresult := parser.ParseFile(\"config.yaml\", &config)\n// Logs: \"yaml_parse\", file: \"config.yaml\", success: true, duration_ms: 15\n```\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe YAML parser module provides a robust, three-layer architecture for YAML handling in ARMOR. The design prioritizes safety, type safety, and debugging support while maintaining performance and extensibility for future enhancements.\n\nThe module follows Go best practices with clear error handling, comprehensive testing, and logical separation of concerns across file I/O, parsing, validation, and field access layers.","numLines":354,"startLine":1,"totalLines":354}}}

The module is designed to handle ARMOR debug files and configuration data with robust error handling and type-safe operations.\n6\t\n7\t## Architecture\n8\t\n9\t### Design Principles\n10\t\n11\t1. **Separation of Concerns**: Each file has a specific responsibility - parsing, validation, file I/O, or field access\n12\t2. **Error Context**: All operations provide detailed error context with line/column information\n13\t3. **Type Safety**: Type-safe field access with clear error messages for type mismatches\n14\t4. **Flexibility**: Support for both struct-based and generic map-based parsing\n15\t5. **Validation First**: Separate validation layer to catch syntax errors before processing\n16\t\n17\t### Module Organization\n18\t\n19\t```\n20\tinternal/yamlutil/\n21\t├── doc.go              # Package documentation and usage examples\n22\t├── file.go             # File I/O operations with contextual error handling\n23\t├── file_test.go        # File operations tests\n24\t├── parser.go           # YAML parsing functionality\n25\t├── parser_test.go      # Parser tests\n26\t├── validator.go        # YAML validation with detailed error reporting\n27\t├── validator_test.go   # Validator tests\n28\t├── debug_helpers.go    # Field access utilities and type-safe helpers\n29\t├── debug_helpers_test.go # Field access tests\n30\t└── (Python equivalents for cross-platform compatibility)\n31\t```\n32\t\n33\t## Core Components\n34\t\n35\t### 1. File Operations Layer (`file.go`)\n36\t\n37\t**Purpose**: Provides safe file I/O operations with enhanced error context.\n38\t\n39\t**Key Types**:\n40\t```go\n41\ttype FileError struct {\n42\t    Op   string // Operation that failed (e.g., \"read\", \"exists\")\n43\t    Path string // File path that caused the error\n44\t    Err  error  // Underlying OS error\n45\t}\n46\t```\n47\t\n48\t**Key Functions**:\n49\t- `ReadFile(filePath string) ([]byte, error)` - Context-aware file reading\n50\t- `FileExists(filePath string) bool` - Safe file existence checking\n51\t- `IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool` - Error type checking\n52\t- `IsPermissionError(err error) bool` - Permission error detection\n53\t\n54\t**Design Features**:\n55\t- Absolute path resolution for better error messages\n56\t- Distinguishes between file not found and permission errors\n57\t- Wraps OS errors with operation context\n58\t\n59\t### 2. Parser Layer (`parser.go`)\n60\t\n61\t**Purpose**: Core YAML parsing functionality with multiple access patterns.\n62\t\n63\t**Key Types**:\n64\t```go\n65\ttype ParseResult struct {\n66\t    FilePath string       // Path to the parsed file\n67\t    Data     interface{}  // Parsed YAML data\n68\t    Success  bool         // Whether parsing succeeded\n69\t    Error    error        // Error if parsing failed\n70\t}\n71\t\n72\ttype Parser struct {\n73\t    strict bool // Strict mode for unknown field rejection\n74\t}\n75\t\n76\ttype YAMLParseError struct {\n77\t    FilePath string\n78\t    Line     int\n79\t    Column   int\n80\t    Message  string\n81\t    RawError error\n82\t}\n83\t```\n84\t\n85\t**Key Functions**:\n86\t- `NewParser() *Parser` - Create standard parser\n87\t- `NewStrictParser() *Parser` - Create strict mode parser\n88\t- `ParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) ParseResult` - Parse into struct\n89\t- `ParseFileToMap(filePath string) ParseResult` - Parse into generic map\n90\t- `ParseYAML(filePath string) (map[string]interface{}, error)` - Simplified parsing\n91\t- `ParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error` - Parse string content\n92\t\n93\t**Design Features**:\n94\t- Handles empty files gracefully (returns empty map, not error)\n95\t- Provides line/column information for syntax errors\n96\t- Supports both typed structs and generic maps\n97\t- Includes file discovery utilities\n98\t\n99\t### 3. Validation Layer (`validator.go`)\n100\t\n101\t**Purpose**: Pre-processing validation to catch syntax and structural errors.\n102\t\n103\t**Key Types**:\n104\t```go\n105\ttype ErrorType string\n106\t\n107\tconst (\n108\t    ErrorTypeSyntax     ErrorType = \"syntax\"\n109\t    ErrorTypeStructure  ErrorType = \"structure\"\n110\t    ErrorTypeIO         ErrorType = \"io\"\n111\t    ErrorTypeEmpty      ErrorType = \"empty\"\n112\t    ErrorTypeDeprecated ErrorType = \"deprecated\"\n113\t    ErrorTypeUnknown    ErrorType = \"unknown\"\n114\t)\n115\t\n116\ttype ValidationError struct {\n117\t    Type     ErrorType\n118\t    Line     int\n119\t    Column   int\n120\t    Message  string\n121\t    Context  string\n122\t    FilePath string\n123\t}\n124\t\n125\ttype ValidationResult struct {\n126\t    FilePath string\n127\t    Valid    bool\n128\t    Errors   []ValidationError\n129\t    Warnings []ValidationError\n130\t}\n131\t\n132\ttype Validator struct {\n133\t    strict bool\n134\t}\n135\t```\n136\t\n137\t**Key Functions**:\n138\t- `NewValidator() *Validator` - Create standard validator\n139\t- `NewStrictValidator() *Validator` - Create strict validator\n140\t- `ValidateFile(filePath string) ValidationResult` - Validate file\n141\t- `ValidateString(yamlContent string) ValidationResult` - Validate string content\n142\t- `ValidateMultipleFiles(filePaths []string) []ValidationResult` - Batch validation\n143\t\n144\t**Design Features**:\n145\t- Categorizes errors by type (syntax, structure, I/O)\n146\t- Provides line/column context for errors\n147\t- Separates errors from warnings\n148\t- Supports both file and string validation\n149\t\n150\t### 4. Field Access Layer (`debug_helpers.go`)\n151\t\n152\t**Purpose**: Type-safe field access with dot notation navigation.\n153\t\n154\t**Key Types**:\n155\t```go\n156\ttype FieldNotFoundError struct {\n157\t    FieldPath string // Dot-separated path to missing field\n158\t}\n159\t\n160\ttype TypeMismatchError struct {\n161\t    FieldPath    string\n162\t    ExpectedType string\n163\t    ActualType   string\n164\t}\n165\t\n166\ttype FieldRequirement struct {\n167\t    Path         string\n168\t    ExpectedType string\n169\t    Optional     bool\n170\t}\n171\t```\n172\t\n173\t**Key Functions**:\n174\t- `GetField(data, path, defaultValue)` - Generic field access\n175\t- `GetString(data, path, defaultValue) string` - String field access\n176\t- `GetInt(data, path, defaultValue) int` - Integer field access  \n177\t- `GetBool(data, path, defaultValue) bool` - Boolean field access\n178\t- `HasField(data, path) bool` - Field existence check\n179\t- `GetRequiredString(data, path) (string, error)` - Required field access\n180\t- `GetRequiredInt(data, path) (int, error)` - Required field access\n181\t- `GetRequiredBool(data, path) (bool, error)` - Required field access\n182\t- `ValidateRequiredFields(data, requiredFields) []string` - Batch validation\n183\t- `ValidateFieldRequirements(data, requirements) []error` - Typed validation\n184\t\n185\t**Design Features**:\n186\t- Dot notation for nested access (e.g., \"server.port\")\n187\t- Type conversion support (string→int, string→bool)\n188\t- Clear error messages for missing/mistyped fields\n189\t- Support for optional vs required fields\n190\t\n191\t## API Design Patterns\n192\t\n193\t### Pattern 1: Struct-Based Parsing\n194\t\n195\t```go\n196\t// Define configuration structure\n197\ttype ServerConfig struct {\n198\t    Host     string `yaml:\"host\"`\n199\t    Port     int    `yaml:\"port\"`\n200\t    Enabled  bool   `yaml:\"enabled\"`\n201\t}\n202\t\n203\t// Parse into struct\n204\tparser := NewParser()\n205\tvar config ServerConfig\n206\tresult := parser.ParseFile(\"config.yaml\", &config)\n207\t\n208\tif !result.Success {\n209\t    log.Fatal(result.Error)\n210\t}\n211\t\n212\t// Use typed configuration\n213\tfmt.Println(\"Server:\", config.Host, config.Port)\n214\t```\n215\t\n216\t### Pattern 2: Generic Map-Based Parsing\n217\t\n218\t```go\n219\t// Parse into generic map\n220\tdata, err := ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n221\tif err != nil {\n222\t    log.Fatal(err)\n223\t}\n224\t\n225\t// Access fields with dot notation\n226\thost := GetString(data, \"server.host\", \"localhost\")\n227\tport := GetInt(data, \"server.port\", 8080)\n228\tenabled := GetBool(data, \"server.enabled\", true)\n229\t```\n230\t\n231\t### Pattern 3: Validation-First Processing\n232\t\n233\t```go\n234\t// Validate first\n235\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n236\tresult := validator.ValidateFile(\"config.yaml\")\n237\t\n238\tif result.HasErrors() {\n239\t    fmt.Println(\"Validation failed:\")\n240\t    for _, err := range result.Errors {\n241\t        fmt.Printf(\"  Line %d: %s\\n\", err.Line, err.Message)\n242\t    }\n243\t    return\n244\t}\n245\t\n246\t// Then process if valid\n247\tif result.HasWarnings() {\n248\t    fmt.Println(\"Warnings:\")\n249\t    for _, warn := range result.Warnings {\n250\t        fmt.Printf(\"  Line %d: %s\\n\", warn.Line, warn.Message)\n251\t    }\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\tdata, _ := ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n255\t```\n256\t\n257\t### Pattern 4: Required Field Validation\n258\t\n259\t```go\n260\t// Parse configuration\n261\tdata, err := ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n262\tif err != nil {\n263\t    log.Fatal(err)\n264\t}\n265\t\n266\t// Define requirements\n267\trequirements := []FieldRequirement{\n268\t    {Path: \"server.host\", ExpectedType: \"string\"},\n269\t    {Path: \"server.port\", ExpectedType: \"int\"},\n270\t    {Path: \"database.name\", ExpectedType: \"string\"},\n271\t    {Path: \"debug.enabled\", ExpectedType: \"bool\", Optional: true},\n272\t}\n273\t\n274\t// Validate requirements\n275\terrors := ValidateFieldRequirements(data, requirements)\n276\tif len(errors) > 0 {\n277\t    fmt.Println(\"Configuration validation failed:\")\n278\t    for _, err := range errors {\n279\t        fmt.Println(\"  \", err.Error())\n280\t    }\n281\t    return\n282\t}\n283\t```\n284\t\n285\t## Error Handling Strategy\n286\t\n287\t### Error Categories\n288\t\n289\t1. **File I/O Errors** (`FileError`)\n290\t   - File not found\n291\t   - Permission denied\n292\t   - Path resolution failures\n293\t\n294\t2. **Parse Errors** (`YAMLParseError`)\n295\t   - Syntax errors\n296\t   - Structure errors\n297\t   - Type conversion failures\n298\t\n299\t3. **Validation Errors** (`ValidationError`)\n300\t   - Categorized by type (syntax, structure, I/O)\n301\t   - Include line/column context\n302\t   - Provide contextual information\n303\t\n304\t4. **Field Access Errors**\n305\t   - `FieldNotFoundError` - Missing required fields\n306\t   - `TypeMismatchError` - Type conversion failures\n307\t\n308\t### Error Handling Best Practices\n309\t\n310\t```go\n311\t// Always check error types for specific handling\n312\tdata, err := ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n313\tif err != nil {\n314\t    if parseErr, ok := err.(*YAMLParseError); ok {\n315\t        fmt.Printf(\"Parse error at line %d: %s\\n\", parseErr.Line, parseErr.Message)\n316\t    } else if fileErr, ok := err.(*FileError); ok {\n317\t        if IsFileNotFoundError(err) {\n318\t            fmt.Println(\"Config file not found\")\n319\t        } else if IsPermissionError(err) {\n320\t            fmt.Println(\"Permission denied\")\n321\t        }\n322\t    }\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\t// Use validation results for detailed error reporting\n326\tresult := validator.ValidateFile(\"config.yaml\")\n327\tif result.HasErrors() {\n328\t    for _, err := range result.Errors {\n329\t        switch err.Type {\n330\t        case ErrorTypeSyntax:\n331\t            fmt.Printf(\"Syntax error: %s\\n\", err.Message)\n332\t        case ErrorTypeStructure:\n333\t            fmt.Printf(\"Structure error: %s\\n\", err.Message)\n334\t        }\n335\t    }\n336\t}\n337\t```\n338\t\n339\t## Integration Points\n340\t\n341\t### 1. ARMOR Debug File Processing\n342\t\n343\tThe module is designed to handle ARMOR debug files with the following characteristics:\n344\t- Complex nested structures\n345\t- Mixed data types\n346\t- Optional and required fields\n347\t- Configuration validation requirements\n348\t\n349\t### 2. Configuration Management\n350\t\n351\t```go\n352\t// Load and validate application configuration\n353\tfunc LoadConfig(path string) (*Config, error) {\n354\t    // Validate first\n355\t    validator := NewValidator()\n356\t    result := validator.ValidateFile(path)\n357\t    if result.HasErrors() {\n358\t        return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"config validation failed: %v\", result.Errors)\n359\t    }\n360\t    \n361\t    // Parse into typed structure\n362\t    parser := NewParser()\n363\t    var config Config\n364\t    parseResult := parser.ParseFile(path, &config)\n365\t    if !parseResult.Success {\n366\t        return nil, parseResult.Error\n367\t    }\n368\t    \n369\t    return &config, nil\n370\t}\n371\t```\n372\t\n373\t### 3. Tool Integration\n374\t\n375\tThe module provides utilities for:\n376\t- Batch validation of configuration files\n377\t- File discovery in configuration directories\n378\t- Debug field access for troubleshooting\n379\t\n380\t## Performance Considerations\n381\t\n382\t1. **Lazy Parsing**: Use `ParseFileToMap` for large files when only partial access is needed\n383\t2. **Validation Caching**: Validation results can be cached for unchanged files\n384\t3. **Batch Operations**: Use `ValidateMultipleFiles` for processing multiple files\n385\t4. **Memory Efficiency**: Generic maps use more memory than structs for complex data\n386\t\n387\t## Testing Strategy\n388\t\n389\t### Unit Tests\n390\t\n391\tEach component has comprehensive test coverage:\n392\t- `file_test.go` - File I/O operations\n393\t- `parser_test.go` - Parsing functionality\n394\t- `validator_test.go` - Validation logic\n395\t- `debug_helpers_test.go` - Field access operations\n396\t\n397\t### Test Categories\n398\t\n399\t1. **Happy Path Tests**: Standard usage patterns\n400\t2. **Error Cases**: Invalid inputs, missing files, malformed YAML\n401\t3. **Edge Cases**: Empty files, special characters, large files\n402\t4. **Type Conversion**: Various data type combinations\n403\t\n404\t## Future Enhancements\n405\t\n406\t### Potential Improvements\n407\t\n408\t1. **Streaming Support**: Add support for streaming large YAML files\n409\t2. **Schema Validation**: Integrate JSON Schema validation\n410\t3. **YAML 1.2 Support**: Upgrade parser for full YAML 1.2 compatibility\n411\t4. **Merge Operations**: Add utilities for merging YAML configurations\n412\t5. **Path Expressions**: Support more complex path expressions (arrays, wildcards)\n413\t6. **Caching Layer**: Add caching for frequently accessed configuration files\n414\t7. **Watch Support**: Add file watching for hot-reload configurations\n415\t8. **Custom Type Converters**: Allow registration of custom type conversion functions\n416\t\n417\t### Extension Points\n418\t\n419\t1. **Custom Validators**: Plugin system for domain-specific validation\n420\t2. **Type Unmarshalers**: Custom YAML unmarshalers for complex types\n421\t3. **Error Handlers**: Configurable error handling strategies\n422\t4. **File Resolvers**: Custom file path resolution logic\n423\t\n424\t## Cross-Platform Support\n425\t\n426\tThe module includes Python equivalents for cross-platform compatibility:\n427\t\n428\t```\n429\tinternal/yamlutil/\n430\t├── __init__.py\n431\t├── validator.py\n432\t├── error_types.py\n433\t```\n434\t\n435\tThis ensures that YAML processing can be performed consistently across different environments and tools in the ARMOR ecosystem.\n436\t\n437\t## Documentation Standards\n438\t\n439\tAll exported functions and types should include:\n440\t- Clear purpose description\n441\t- Parameter descriptions\n442\t- Return value explanations\n443\t- Usage examples\n444\t- Error condition descriptions\n445\t\n446\tPackage-level documentation should include:\n447\t- Module overview\n448\t- Design philosophy\n449\t- Usage patterns\n450\t- Integration examples\n451\t- Error handling guidelines\n452\t\n453\t## Conclusion\n454\t\n455\tThe YAML Parser Module provides a robust, type-safe foundation for YAML processing in ARMOR. Its layered architecture, comprehensive error handling, and flexible API design make it suitable for both simple configuration parsing and complex debug file processing tasks.\n456\t\n457\tThe module's design prioritizes:\n458\t- **Safety**: Type checking and validation prevent runtime errors\n459\t- **Clarity**: Clear error messages and context aid debugging\n460\t- **Flexibility**: Multiple access patterns for different use cases\n461\t- **Maintainability**: Clear separation of concerns and comprehensive testing\n462\t\n463\tThis design document serves as both architectural documentation and a guide for future enhancements and integration work."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"e6198b15-e2e1-467d-a9ec-23d201c401d5","uuid":"68262341-d1d5-4446-a078-496ec1b29009","timestamp":"2026-07-09T15:52:34.176Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/docs/yaml-module-design.md","content":"# YAML Parser Module Design\n\n## Overview\n\nThe YAML Parser Module (`internal/yamlutil`) provides comprehensive YAML parsing, validation, and field access utilities for the ARMOR project. The module is designed to handle ARMOR debug files and configuration data with robust error handling and type-safe operations.\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Design Principles\n\n1. **Separation of Concerns**: Each file has a specific responsibility - parsing, validation, file I/O, or field access\n2. **Error Context**: All operations provide detailed error context with line/column information\n3. **Type Safety**: Type-safe field access with clear error messages for type mismatches\n4. **Flexibility**: Support for both struct-based and generic map-based parsing\n5. **Validation First**: Separate validation layer to catch syntax errors before processing\n\n### Module Organization\n\n```\ninternal/yamlutil/\n├── doc.go              # Package documentation and usage examples\n├── file.go             # File I/O operations with contextual error handling\n├── file_test.go        # File operations tests\n├── parser.go           # YAML parsing functionality\n├── parser_test.go      # Parser tests\n├── validator.go        # YAML validation with detailed error reporting\n├── validator_test.go   # Validator tests\n├── debug_helpers.go    # Field access utilities and type-safe helpers\n├── debug_helpers_test.go # Field access tests\n└── (Python equivalents for cross-platform compatibility)\n```\n\n## Core Components\n\n### 1. File Operations Layer (`file.go`)\n\n**Purpose**: Provides safe file I/O operations with enhanced error context.\n\n**Key Types**:\n```go\ntype FileError struct {\n    Op   string // Operation that failed (e.g., \"read\", \"exists\")\n    Path string // File path that caused the error\n    Err  error  // Underlying OS error\n}\n```\n\n**Key Functions**:\n- `ReadFile(filePath string) ([]byte, error)` - Context-aware file reading\n- `FileExists(filePath string) bool` - Safe file existence checking\n- `IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool` - Error type checking\n- `IsPermissionError(err error) bool` - Permission error detection\n\n**Design Features**:\n- Absolute path resolution for better error messages\n- Distinguishes between file not found and permission errors\n- Wraps OS errors with operation context\n\n### 2. Parser Layer (`parser.go`)\n\n**Purpose**: Core YAML parsing functionality with multiple access patterns.\n\n**Key Types**:\n```go\ntype ParseResult struct {\n    FilePath string       // Path to the parsed file\n    Data     interface{}  // Parsed YAML data\n    Success  bool         // Whether parsing succeeded\n    Error    error        // Error if parsing failed\n}\n\ntype Parser struct {\n    strict bool // Strict mode for unknown field rejection\n}\n\ntype YAMLParseError struct {\n    FilePath string\n    Line     int\n    Column   int\n    Message  string\n    RawError error\n}\n```\n\n**Key Functions**:\n- `NewParser() *Parser` - Create standard parser\n- `NewStrictParser() *Parser` - Create strict mode parser\n- `ParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) ParseResult` - Parse into struct\n- `ParseFileToMap(filePath string) ParseResult` - Parse into generic map\n- `ParseYAML(filePath string) (map[string]interface{}, error)` - Simplified parsing\n- `ParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error` - Parse string content\n\n**Design Features**:\n- Handles empty files gracefully (returns empty map, not error)\n- Provides line/column information for syntax errors\n- Supports both typed structs and generic maps\n- Includes file discovery utilities\n\n### 3. Validation Layer (`validator.go`)\n\n**Purpose**: Pre-processing validation to catch syntax and structural errors.\n\n**Key Types**:\n```go\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n    ErrorTypeSyntax     ErrorType = \"syntax\"\n    ErrorTypeStructure  ErrorType = \"structure\"\n    ErrorTypeIO         ErrorType = \"io\"\n    ErrorTypeEmpty      ErrorType = \"empty\"\n    ErrorTypeDeprecated ErrorType = \"deprecated\"\n    ErrorTypeUnknown    ErrorType = \"unknown\"\n)\n\ntype ValidationError struct {\n    Type     ErrorType\n    Line     int\n    Column   int\n    Message  string\n    Context  string\n    FilePath string\n}\n\ntype ValidationResult struct {\n    FilePath string\n    Valid    bool\n    Errors   []ValidationError\n    Warnings []ValidationError\n}\n\ntype Validator struct {\n    strict bool\n}\n```\n\n**Key Functions**:\n- `NewValidator() *Validator` - Create standard validator\n- `NewStrictValidator() *Validator` - Create strict validator\n- `ValidateFile(filePath string) ValidationResult` - Validate file\n- `ValidateString(yamlContent string) ValidationResult` - Validate string content\n- `ValidateMultipleFiles(filePaths []string) []ValidationResult` - Batch validation\n\n**Design Features**:\n- Categorizes errors by type (syntax, structure, I/O)\n- Provides line/column context for errors\n- Separates errors from warnings\n- Supports both file and string validation\n\n### 4. Field Access Layer (`debug_helpers.go`)\n\n**Purpose**: Type-safe field access with dot notation navigation.\n\n**Key Types**:\n```go\ntype FieldNotFoundError struct {\n    FieldPath string // Dot-separated path to missing field\n}\n\ntype TypeMismatchError struct {\n    FieldPath    string\n    ExpectedType string\n    ActualType   string\n}\n\ntype FieldRequirement struct {\n    Path         string\n    ExpectedType string\n    Optional     bool\n}\n```\n\n**Key Functions**:\n- `GetField(data, path, defaultValue)` - Generic field access\n- `GetString(data, path, defaultValue) string` - String field access\n- `GetInt(data, path, defaultValue) int` - Integer field access  \n- `GetBool(data, path, defaultValue) bool` - Boolean field access\n- `HasField(data, path) bool` - Field existence check\n- `GetRequiredString(data, path) (string, error)` - Required field access\n- `GetRequiredInt(data, path) (int, error)` - Required field access\n- `GetRequiredBool(data, path) (bool, error)` - Required field access\n- `ValidateRequiredFields(data, requiredFields) []string` - Batch validation\n- `ValidateFieldRequirements(data, requirements) []error` - Typed validation\n\n**Design Features**:\n- Dot notation for nested access (e.g., \"server.port\")\n- Type conversion support (string→int, string→bool)\n- Clear error messages for missing/mistyped fields\n- Support for optional vs required fields\n\n## API Design Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Struct-Based Parsing\n\n```go\n// Define configuration structure\ntype ServerConfig struct {\n    Host     string `yaml:\"host\"`\n    Port     int    `yaml:\"port\"`\n    Enabled  bool   `yaml:\"enabled\"`\n}\n\n// Parse into struct\nparser := NewParser()\nvar config ServerConfig\nresult := parser.ParseFile(\"config.yaml\", &config)\n\nif !result.Success {\n    log.Fatal(result.Error)\n}\n\n// Use typed configuration\nfmt.Println(\"Server:\", config.Host, config.Port)\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Generic Map-Based Parsing\n\n```go\n// Parse into generic map\ndata, err := ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\nif err != nil {\n    log.Fatal(err)\n}\n\n// Access fields with dot notation\nhost := GetString(data, \"server.host\", \"localhost\")\nport := GetInt(data, \"server.port\", 8080)\nenabled := GetBool(data, \"server.enabled\", true)\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: Validation-First Processing\n\n```go\n// Validate first\nvalidator := NewValidator()\nresult := validator.ValidateFile(\"config.yaml\")\n\nif result.HasErrors() {\n    fmt.Println(\"Validation failed:\")\n    for _, err := range result.Errors {\n        fmt.Printf(\"  Line %d: %s\\n\", err.Line, err.Message)\n    }\n    return\n}\n\n// Then process if valid\nif result.HasWarnings() {\n    fmt.Println(\"Warnings:\")\n    for _, warn := range result.Warnings {\n        fmt.Printf(\"  Line %d: %s\\n\", warn.Line, warn.Message)\n    }\n}\n\ndata, _ := ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n```\n\n### Pattern 4: Required Field Validation\n\n```go\n// Parse configuration\ndata, err := ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\nif err != nil {\n    log.Fatal(err)\n}\n\n// Define requirements\nrequirements := []FieldRequirement{\n    {Path: \"server.host\", ExpectedType: \"string\"},\n    {Path: \"server.port\", ExpectedType: \"int\"},\n    {Path: \"database.name\", ExpectedType: \"string\"},\n    {Path: \"debug.enabled\", ExpectedType: \"bool\", Optional: true},\n}\n\n// Validate requirements\nerrors := ValidateFieldRequirements(data, requirements)\nif len(errors) > 0 {\n    fmt.Println(\"Configuration validation failed:\")\n    for _, err := range errors {\n        fmt.Println(\"  \", err.Error())\n    }\n    return\n}\n```\n\n## Error Handling Strategy\n\n### Error Categories\n\n1. **File I/O Errors** (`FileError`)\n   - File not found\n   - Permission denied\n   - Path resolution failures\n\n2. **Parse Errors** (`YAMLParseError`)\n   - Syntax errors\n   - Structure errors\n   - Type conversion failures\n\n3. **Validation Errors** (`ValidationError`)\n   - Categorized by type (syntax, structure, I/O)\n   - Include line/column context\n   - Provide contextual information\n\n4. **Field Access Errors**\n   - `FieldNotFoundError` - Missing required fields\n   - `TypeMismatchError` - Type conversion failures\n\n### Error Handling Best Practices\n\n```go\n// Always check error types for specific handling\ndata, err := ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\nif err != nil {\n    if parseErr, ok := err.(*YAMLParseError); ok {\n        fmt.Printf(\"Parse error at line %d: %s\\n\", parseErr.Line, parseErr.Message)\n    } else if fileErr, ok := err.(*FileError); ok {\n        if IsFileNotFoundError(err) {\n            fmt.Println(\"Config file not found\")\n        } else if IsPermissionError(err) {\n            fmt.Println(\"Permission denied\")\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Use validation results for detailed error reporting\nresult := validator.ValidateFile(\"config.yaml\")\nif result.HasErrors() {\n    for _, err := range result.Errors {\n        switch err.Type {\n        case ErrorTypeSyntax:\n            fmt.Printf(\"Syntax error: %s\\n\", err.Message)\n        case ErrorTypeStructure:\n            fmt.Printf(\"Structure error: %s\\n\", err.Message)\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n## Integration Points\n\n### 1. ARMOR Debug File Processing\n\nThe module is designed to handle ARMOR debug files with the following characteristics:\n- Complex nested structures\n- Mixed data types\n- Optional and required fields\n- Configuration validation requirements\n\n### 2. Configuration Management\n\n```go\n// Load and validate application configuration\nfunc LoadConfig(path string) (*Config, error) {\n    // Validate first\n    validator := NewValidator()\n    result := validator.ValidateFile(path)\n    if result.HasErrors() {\n        return nil, fmt.Errorf(\"config validation failed: %v\", result.Errors)\n    }\n    \n    // Parse into typed structure\n    parser := NewParser()\n    var config Config\n    parseResult := parser.ParseFile(path, &config)\n    if !parseResult.Success {\n        return nil, parseResult.Error\n    }\n    \n    return &config, nil\n}\n```\n\n### 3. Tool Integration\n\nThe module provides utilities for:\n- Batch validation of configuration files\n- File discovery in configuration directories\n- Debug field access for troubleshooting\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n1. **Lazy Parsing**: Use `ParseFileToMap` for large files when only partial access is needed\n2. **Validation Caching**: Validation results can be cached for unchanged files\n3. **Batch Operations**: Use `ValidateMultipleFiles` for processing multiple files\n4. **Memory Efficiency**: Generic maps use more memory than structs for complex data\n\n## Testing Strategy\n\n### Unit Tests\n\nEach component has comprehensive test coverage:\n- `file_test.go` - File I/O operations\n- `parser_test.go` - Parsing functionality\n- `validator_test.go` - Validation logic\n- `debug_helpers_test.go` - Field access operations\n\n### Test Categories\n\n1. **Happy Path Tests**: Standard usage patterns\n2. **Error Cases**: Invalid inputs, missing files, malformed YAML\n3. **Edge Cases**: Empty files, special characters, large files\n4. **Type Conversion**: Various data type combinations\n\n## Future Enhancements\n\n### Potential Improvements\n\n1. **Streaming Support**: Add support for streaming large YAML files\n2. **Schema Validation**: Integrate JSON Schema validation\n3. **YAML 1.2 Support**: Upgrade parser for full YAML 1.2 compatibility\n4. **Merge Operations**: Add utilities for merging YAML configurations\n5. **Path Expressions**: Support more complex path expressions (arrays, wildcards)\n6. **Caching Layer**: Add caching for frequently accessed configuration files\n7. **Watch Support**: Add file watching for hot-reload configurations\n8. **Custom Type Converters**: Allow registration of custom type conversion functions\n\n### Extension Points\n\n1. **Custom Validators**: Plugin system for domain-specific validation\n2. **Type Unmarshalers**: Custom YAML unmarshalers for complex types\n3. **Error Handlers**: Configurable error handling strategies\n4. **File Resolvers**: Custom file path resolution logic\n\n## Cross-Platform Support\n\nThe module includes Python equivalents for cross-platform compatibility:\n\n```\ninternal/yamlutil/\n├── __init__.py\n├── validator.py\n├── error_types.py\n```\n\nThis ensures that YAML processing can be performed consistently across different environments and tools in the ARMOR ecosystem.\n\n## Documentation Standards\n\nAll exported functions and types should include:\n- Clear purpose description\n- Parameter descriptions\n- Return value explanations\n- Usage examples\n- Error condition descriptions\n\nPackage-level documentation should include:\n- Module overview\n- Design philosophy\n- Usage patterns\n- Integration examples\n- Error handling guidelines\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe YAML Parser Module provides a robust, type-safe foundation for YAML processing in ARMOR. Its layered architecture, comprehensive error handling, and flexible API design make it suitable for both simple configuration parsing and complex debug file processing tasks.\n\nThe module's design prioritizes:\n- **Safety**: Type checking and validation prevent runtime errors\n- **Clarity**: Clear error messages and context aid debugging\n- **Flexibility**: Multiple access patterns for different use cases\n- **Maintainability**: Clear separation of concerns and comprehensive testing\n\nThis design document serves as both architectural documentation and a guide for future enhancements and integration work.","numLines":463,"startLine":1,"totalLines":463}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_ca010dc76765426da23e7795","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# YAML Parser Module Structure\n2\t\n3\t## Overview\n4\t\n5\tThe YAML parser module (`internal/yamlutil/`) provides comprehensive YAML parsing, validation, and field access utilities for ARMOR. The module is designed for robust YAML handling in Go applications with three main components:\n6\t\n7\t1. **File I/O Operations** - Safe, error-contextualized YAML file operations\n8\t2. **YAML Parsing** - Parse YAML files into typed structures or generic maps  \n9\t3. **Field Access** - Type-safe helpers for accessing nested YAML fields\n10\t4. **Validation** - YAML syntax and structure validation with detailed error reporting\n11\t\n12\t## Architecture\n13\t\n14\t```\n15\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n16\t│                     yamlutil Package                        │\n17\t├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n18\t│                                                              │\n19\t│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐     │\n20\t│  │   File I/O   │  │   Parser     │  │  Validator   │     │\n21\t│  │   Module     │  │   Module     │  │   Module     │     │\n22\t│  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘     │\n23\t│                                                              │\n24\t│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐      │\n25\t│  │         Field Access / Debug Helpers              │      │\n26\t│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      │\n27\t│                                                              │\n28\t│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┐      │\n29\t│  │            Error Types & Interfaces               │      │\n30\t│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      │\n31\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n32\t```\n33\t\n34\t## Module Components\n35\t\n36\t### 1. File I/O Module (`file.go`)\n37\t\n38\t**Purpose**: Provide safe file operations with comprehensive error handling\n39\t\n40\t**Key Types**:\n41\t- `FileError` - File operation errors with context\n42\t- `ReadFile` - Read file contents with error context\n43\t- `FileExists` - Check file existence with permission handling\n44\t- `IsFileNotFoundError` - Error type checking\n45\t- `IsPermissionError` - Error type checking\n46\t\n47\t**Design Patterns**:\n48\t- Error wrapping for context preservation\n49\t- Permission-aware file existence checking\n50\t- Absolute path resolution for better error messages\n51\t\n52\t### 2. Parser Module (`parser.go`)\n53\t\n54\t**Purpose**: Parse YAML files with flexible data structures\n55\t\n56\t**Key Types**:\n57\t- `Parser` - YAML parser with strict mode support\n58\t- `ParseResult` - Structured parsing results with metadata\n59\t- `YAMLParseError` - Detailed syntax errors with line/column info\n60\t- `NewParser()` - Standard parser\n61\t- `NewStrictParser()` - Strict mode parser\n62\t\n63\t**Core Methods**:\n64\t```go\n65\tfunc (p *Parser) ParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) ParseResult\n66\tfunc (p *Parser) ParseFileToMap(filePath string) ParseResult\n67\tfunc (p *Parser) ParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error\n68\tfunc (p *Parser) MustParseFile(filePath string, data interface{})\n69\t```\n70\t\n71\t**Design Patterns**:\n72\t- Structured result objects for error handling\n73\t- Generic and typed parsing options\n74\t- Graceful handling of empty files\n75\t- Line/column error extraction\n76\t\n77\t### 3. Validator Module (`validator.go`)\n78\t\n79\t**Purpose**: Validate YAML syntax and structure with detailed reporting\n80\t\n81\t**Key Types**:\n82\t- `Validator` - YAML validator with strict mode\n83\t- `ValidationResult` - Structured validation results\n84\t- `ValidationError` - Detailed error information\n85\t- `ErrorType` - Categorized error types (syntax, structure, I/O, etc.)\n86\t\n87\t**Error Types**:\n88\t- `ErrorTypeSyntax` - YAML syntax errors\n89\t- `ErrorTypeStructure` - Structural errors (duplicate keys, nesting)\n90\t- `ErrorTypeIO` - I/O errors (file not found, permissions)\n91\t- `ErrorTypeEmpty` - Empty file or content\n92\t- `ErrorTypeDeprecated` - Deprecated YAML features\n93\t\n94\t**Core Methods**:\n95\t```go\n96\tfunc (v *Validator) ValidateFile(filePath string) ValidationResult\n97\tfunc (v *Validator) ValidateString(yamlContent string) ValidationResult\n98\tfunc (v *Validator) ValidateMultipleFiles(filePaths []string) []ValidationResult\n99\t```\n100\t\n101\t### 4. Field Access Module (`debug_helpers.go`)\n102\t\n103\t**Purpose**: Type-safe field access with dot notation\n104\t\n105\t**Key Functions**:\n106\t- `GetField` - Generic field access with defaults\n107\t- `GetString, GetInt, GetBool` - Type-safe field access\n108\t- `HasField` - Field existence checking\n109\t- `GetRequiredField, GetRequiredString, GetRequiredInt, GetRequiredBool` - Required field access\n110\t\n111\t**Error Types**:\n112\t- `FieldNotFoundError` - Missing required fields\n113\t- `TypeMismatchError` - Type validation failures\n114\t\n115\t**Design Patterns**:\n116\t- Dot notation for nested access (`server.port`)\n117\t- Type conversion with fallback\n118\t- Required vs optional field handling\n119\t- Batch field validation\n120\t\n121\t## Interface Definitions\n122\t\n123\t### Parser Interface\n124\t\n125\t```go\n126\ttype YAMLParser interface {\n127\t    ParseFile(filePath string, data interface{}) ParseResult\n128\t    ParseFileToMap(filePath string) ParseResult\n129\t    ParseString(yamlContent string, data interface{}) error\n130\t    MustParseFile(filePath string, data interface{})\n131\t}\n132\t```\n133\t\n134\t### Validator Interface\n135\t\n136\t```go\n137\ttype YAMLValidator interface {\n138\t    ValidateFile(filePath string) ValidationResult\n139\t    ValidateString(yamlContent string) ValidationResult\n140\t    ValidateMultipleFiles(filePaths []string) []ValidationResult\n141\t}\n142\t```\n143\t\n144\t### Field Accessor Interface\n145\t\n146\t```go\n147\ttype FieldAccessor interface {\n148\t    GetField(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue interface{}) interface{}\n149\t    GetString(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue string) string\n150\t    GetInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue int) int\n151\t    GetBool(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue bool) bool\n152\t    HasField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) bool\n153\t}\n154\t```\n155\t\n156\t## File Organization\n157\t\n158\t```\n159\tinternal/yamlutil/\n160\t├── doc.go              # Package documentation and usage examples\n161\t├── file.go             # File I/O operations\n162\t├── file_test.go        # File operation tests\n163\t├── parser.go           # YAML parsing implementation\n164\t├── parser_test.go      # Parser tests\n165\t├── validator.go        # YAML validation implementation  \n166\t├── validator_test.go   # Validator tests\n167\t├── debug_helpers.go    # Field access helpers\n168\t├── debug_helpers_test.go # Field access tests\n169\t└── types.go            # Common type definitions (future)\n170\t```\n171\t\n172\t## API Design Principles\n173\t\n174\t### 1. Error Handling\n175\t- All errors include context (file path, operation, line numbers)\n176\t- Specific error types for different failure modes\n177\t- Error unwrapping support for inspection\n178\t\n179\t### 2. Type Safety\n180\t- Both generic (map) and typed (struct) parsing\n181\t- Type-safe field access with automatic conversion\n182\t- Type validation with detailed mismatch information\n183\t\n184\t### 3. Flexibility\n185\t- Support for both strict and lenient parsing/validation\n186\t- Optional vs required field handling\n187\t- Default values for missing fields\n188\t\n189\t### 4. Performance\n190\t- Minimal memory allocations\n191\t- Efficient field navigation\n192\t- Batch operations for multiple files\n193\t\n194\t## Usage Patterns\n195\t\n196\t### Basic File Reading\n197\t```go\n198\tcontent, err := yamlutil.ReadFile(\"config.yaml\")\n199\tif err != nil {\n200\t    if yamlutil.IsFileNotFoundError(err) {\n201\t        log.Fatal(\"Config file not found\")\n202\t    }\n203\t    log.Fatal(err)\n204\t}\n205\t```\n206\t\n207\t### Structured Parsing\n208\t```go\n209\tparser := yamlutil.NewParser()\n210\tvar config Config\n211\tresult := parser.ParseFile(\"config.yaml\", &config)\n212\tif !result.Success {\n213\t    log.Fatal(result.Error)\n214\t}\n215\t```\n216\t\n217\t### Field Access\n218\t```go\n219\tdata, err := yamlutil.ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n220\tif err != nil {\n221\t    log.Fatal(err)\n222\t}\n223\t\n224\t// Type-safe field access with defaults\n225\tport := yamlutil.GetInt(data, \"server.port\", 8080)\n226\tenabled := yamlutil.GetBool(data, \"server.enabled\", true)\n227\t\n228\t// Required fields\n229\ttimeout, err := yamlutil.GetRequiredInt(data, \"server.timeout\")\n230\tif err != nil {\n231\t    log.Fatal(\"Missing required field: server.timeout\")\n232\t}\n233\t```\n234\t\n235\t### Validation\n236\t```go\n237\tvalidator := yamlutil.NewValidator()\n238\tresult :=
